title: Add Two Numbers Within 20
description: Compute the sum of two whole numbers within 20.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Includes counting on or derived facts.

title: Identify Equivalent Representation of a Number
description: Recognize whether a visual or symbolic representation equals a given number.
condition: Given a target number (e.g., 17) and multiple representations (equations, ten‑frames, cubes).
response: Select the representations equal to the target number.
label: Production / Skill
example: Recognize $10 + 7$, $19 - 2$, and a double ten‑frame showing 17 as representations of 17.
notes: Requires evaluating expressions and counting visuals.

title: Represent Teen Number as Ten and Ones
description: Understand a teen number as 10 and some ones.
label: Principle

title: Solve Add-To, Start Unknown Problem
description: Find the starting quantity in a result-unknown addition story.
condition: Story where start + change = result, start unknown (e.g., some kids + 6 = 15).
response: Subtract change from result to find start.
label: Principle

title: Subtract Within 20
description: Compute the difference of two whole numbers within 20.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Includes counting back or known facts.

title: Use Addition to Solve Subtraction
description: Use a known addition fact to determine a related subtraction fact.
condition: Subtraction expression $a - b$ with known related fact $c + b = a$.
label: Principle

title: Use Make‑a‑Ten Strategy for Addition
description: Decompose an addend to make 10 before completing addition.
condition: Addition $a + b$ where composing 10 is helpful (e.g., 6 + 8).
response: Break apart one addend to make 10, then add the remainder.
label: Principle
example: For $6 + 8$: take $4$ from $8$ to make $10$, then $10 + 4 = 14$.
notes: Relies on understanding of 10 as benchmark.

title: Use Make‑a‑Ten Strategy for Subtraction
description: Decompose the subtrahend to subtract to 10 first, then subtract the rest.
condition: Subtraction $a - b$ where bridging to 10 is efficient (e.g., 13 − 5, 15 − 6).
response: Subtract enough to reach 10, then subtract remaining amount.
label: Principle
